programme.title: Mighty Planes
programme.desc: Baffinland Mines needs to get equipment to a remote mine in Nunavut, to support shipping operations for the summer; they hire one of Volga-Dnepr's Ilyushin-76.

programme.title: Mighty Trains
programme.desc: Japan's Tokaido Shinkansen, known as the bullet train, operates on a dedicated railway line at up to 177 mph.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Mighty Cruise Ships
programme.desc: Experience romance on a mega cruiser that pampers its guests with the best of Italian design and American convenience.

programme.title: Mighty Cruise Ships
programme.desc: The world's latest, most-advanced expedition cruise ship Roald Amundsen whisks passengers on an adventurous voyage to Antarctica.

programme.title: Mighty Trains
programme.desc: A trip aboard one of the world's most expensive and sought-after train rides details the journey for both passengers and crew.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Mighty Trains
programme.desc: The prairies of Canada; the deserts of Australia; the cold of the Norwegian Arctic Circle; the steepest slopes of the mighty Swiss Alps.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Mighty Cruise Ships
programme.desc: Japan's largest cruise ship brings East and West together on a cruise featuring cherry.

programme.title: Mighty Cruise Ships
programme.desc: We hop aboard a seven-day cruise to the Canary Islands and Madeira aboard the first large liner to run purely on cleaner-burning liquefied natural gas.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Mighty Cruise Ships
programme.desc: Sailing from Chile and Peru to Ecuador and Costa Rica, the luxurious Seven Seas Explorer cruise ship is full of marble, granite and almost 500 chandeliers.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Mighty Cruise Ships
programme.desc: Norwegian Joy sails amid icebergs in Alaska's Inside Passage, treating guests to glaciers, humpback whales, and the largest zip line in the world.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: A museum building bends 1,100-glass tubes to form a facade and tunnels below the water table; a vast 11-storey glass atrium HQ is built on the former Berlin Wall site to represent unity.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: The American Museum of Natural History in NYC gets a cave-like extension; an arts center inspired by migrating birds is built on a lake in China; Boston University gains a fossil-free, Jenga-like tower.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: A 1.2-million-square-foot office building balances precariously on a 39-foot wide base in Chicago; a building named Pterodactyl lands on top of a Los Angeles parking garage; the world's longest suspension footbridge.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: Examine the sightlines in Los Angeles's SoFi Stadium, which sits right in the flight path of LAX; celebrate the ingenuity of the inexperienced crew that built a monumental skyscraper in Malta; visit a forest tower designed for testing elevators.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: Challenges beset a sculptural new transportation hub for New York's World Trade Center; Amsterdam gains a building inspired by a mountain valley; a new launchpad for space travel prepares for liftoff in the New Mexico desert.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: A sculptural high-rise with rippling balconies breaks the mold in Chicago; Mammoth machines bore 26 miles of tunnels for an underground railroad beneath London's city streets.

programme.title: How Did They Fix That?
programme.desc: In the Alps of Switzerland, Mike helps an elite team install a new cable (rope) system on an iconic mountain gondola line, helps fix the world's steepest cogwheel railway track and explores mountaintop family farms only accessib.

programme.title: How Did They Fix That?
programme.desc: Mike joins crews as they build the world's longest railway tunnel in the Alps, fixing gridlock through the infamous Brenner Pass by drilling, blasting and digging their way through the mountains.

programme.title: How Did They Fix That?
programme.desc: Host Mike Davidson goes to the remote Atacama desert in Chile; at observatory ALMA he works alongside the expert team as they repair some of the world's biggest antennas.

programme.title: How Did They Fix That?
programme.sub-title: Fixing For A Fight: U.S. Army Mega Repair Base
programme.desc: Mike works with the U.S. Army in Germany inside the massive fix facility The MAK, Maintenance Activity Kaiserslautern, as teams repair over one hundred Bradley fighting vehicles, then embeds with troops in a massive two week long battle exercise.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: Visiting Australia's One Central Park, the Oslo Opera House and the California Academy of Sciences in San Francisco to reveal their architectural secrets.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: An unstable high rise, an otherworldly museum, and a towering cliffside elevator.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: A 284ft wooden skyscraper built from 1,200 bespoke pieces tests fire safety limits in Milwaukee; a decaying Amsterdam crane track transforms into glass offices; a needle-thin tower stacks 28 apartments onto a 21ft-wide plot in Melbourne, Australia.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: Paying tribute to the brave, gravity-defying design of the U.S. National Medal of Honor Museum; traveling to Singapore for a look at a leaning tower of gambling, then exploring a New York City office building that lets the sun shine through.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: Celebrating one of humanities biggest engineering achievements, the International Space Station, a meteor proof lab bigger than a football field, constructed by astronauts traveling 250 miles above us at 17,500 miles an hour.

programme.title: Disasters Engineered
programme.desc: A 1966 landslide in Wales killed 116 children and 28 adults; a 2019 dam collapse killed 115 people in Brazil.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: History's most terrifying air disasters, and the investigations that followed, are revisited and reexamined.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A look at the fate of United Airlines Flight 232 which crashed into Sioux City's runway in 1989; it caused a subsequent explosion that left 111 people dead.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Seeing how international investigative teams must overcome differences to solve controversial plane crashes.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Investigating the 2009 crash of Colgan Air Flight 3407; the plane stalls at low altitude and dives into a residential area near Buffalo, N.Y.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Moments after takeoff, a plane sustains electrical failures and crashes in the Florida Everglades, killing 110 people on board.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Investigators struggle to determine why a 757 strayed off course during its final approach to a Colombia airport and crashed into a mountain.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: When a British tycoon and his family die in a 2017 Australian seaplane crash, investigators spend over two years searching for the reason behind the tragedy.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A plane carrying Mexico's interior minister crashes into Mexico City's financial district.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: The 200-year evolution of the submarine in wartime is examined, told by the designers and engineers behind the machines.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: An up-close look at the naval carriers, destroyers, and nuclear submarines of the Cold War.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: Stories of historic Black struggle for equality in maritime combat; an enslaved powder boy who served during the French and Indian War; a Civil War gunner; the first African American to command a vessel with a fully integrated crew: Hugh Mulzac.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A look at how technological advances over the centuries have led to bigger and faster combat ships and transformed maritime warfare.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A look at key naval battles that have taken place in the Persian Gulf over the last 50 years and the warships involved, from both sides of the conflicts.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A look at the smallest ships of war and the major roles they've played over the centuries, including the 1776 gunboat built to stop a British invasion and the HA-19 sub.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: New high-tech investigations uncover what sunk a World War I cruiser, a World War II submarine, a British destroyer from the Falklands War, and more.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A look at how navy weapons have evolved over the centuries, from cannons to gun turrets to nuclear missiles and beyond.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: Exploring maritime heroes who left their mark on history through their bravery and skill, including Larry Chambers, Ernest Evans, and Robert Smalls.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A look at riverine warfare from the Civil War to Vietnam and small but powerful combat ships.

programme.desc: The inside story of the massive military transport aircraft, the C-17.

programme.desc: The history of the F-16 Fighting Falcon and how it became the U.S. Air Force's most versatile plane.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: A countdown of the ten greatest military planes of World War II, including the Japanese Zero, the Nazi ME 109, and the B-29 Superfortress.

programme.desc: The inside story of the F-4 Phantom, the U.S. military's go-to fighter for more than four decades.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: An inside look at the U.S. Air Force's largest aircraft, the C-5 Galaxy; its most dramatic combat and peacetime missions over the last 50 years.
